title: Daily Standup — Week 08 (6 weeks ago / mid-period)

utterances.speaker: maria
utterances.text: Morning everyone! Marina's on PTO this week so I'll run standup. Ivan, kick us off?

utterances.speaker: ivan
utterances.text: Sure. Yesterday finished the session cache refactor — finally. It was messy but I think we got it. PR is up for review. Also been looking at the token refresh flow, I think there's a subtle race condition we haven't hit yet but it worries me.

utterances.speaker: maria
utterances.text: Good catch on the race condition. Can you write up the scenario? I'll pair with you to think through the fix.

utterances.speaker: ivan
utterances.text: Yeah, I'll write it up today. It's in _validate_session, line 84. When two concurrent requests come in during token refresh, there's no lock. We got lucky so far.

utterances.speaker: maria
utterances.text: Classic TOCTOU. Let's tackle it before it bites us in prod. I'll block 2-3pm for us.

utterances.speaker: ivan
utterances.text: Thanks. That works.

utterances.speaker: tom
utterances.text: I picked up TASK-10, validator refactor. Almost done, should have a PR today. Ivan, I might need your input on the auth endpoint validation rules — is there a doc?

utterances.speaker: ivan
utterances.text: Not really, it's mostly in my head. Let's sync after standup for 15 min, I'll walk you through it.

utterances.speaker: tom
utterances.text: Perfect, thank you. Also — rate limiting spec from last week, ready to start coding. Any objections to my approach in the design doc?

utterances.speaker: maria
utterances.text: Design doc looks solid. One thing — consider token bucket over sliding window for auth endpoints specifically. Easier to tune. But either works.

utterances.speaker: tom
utterances.text: Good point, I'll use token bucket. Maria always has the better algorithm.

utterances.speaker: anna
utterances.text: Frontend update — graph view interactions are looking great after the 3D lib upgrade. Working with Nikita on the component structure. Nikita, how'd the LayerToggle work go yesterday?

utterances.speaker: nikita
utterances.text: Um, it was challenging! I rewrote it three times before it clicked. @anna your comment about uncontrolled vs controlled components made it make sense. I think it's working now?

utterances.speaker: anna
utterances.text: That's the learning curve right there — good job pushing through. Let's review it together at 11.

utterances.speaker: nikita
utterances.text: Yes! I'll have it ready. Also a question — is it normal to feel like you don't know anything even after 3 weeks?

utterances.speaker: maria
utterances.text: That's called imposter syndrome and it means you're paying attention. 3 weeks is nothing, give yourself 3 months.

utterances.speaker: ivan
utterances.text: Still feels like that after 18 months sometimes, fwiw.

utterances.speaker: anna
utterances.text: What Ivan said. You're doing great Nikita.

utterances.speaker: peter
utterances.text: QA update: integration tests for the session cache changes are passing. Ivan's PR looks good from a test coverage standpoint — 82% on the new code. One question: the concurrent request test in test_auth.py — is that intentionally commented out?

utterances.speaker: ivan
utterances.text: Yeah, I commented it out because it was flaky due to timing. With the race condition fix we talked about, it should be reliable. Let's uncomment it once that's done.

utterances.speaker: peter
utterances.text: Got it. I'll create a tracking ticket for that.

utterances.speaker: maria
utterances.text: Good. Any blockers? Looks like a solid week shaping up.

utterances.speaker: tom
utterances.text: Just the auth sync with Ivan, which is happening in 15 min. No blockers after that.

utterances.speaker: maria
utterances.text: Great. Everyone ship safe, ping if you need unblocking help. Ivan and I are pairing at 2pm on the race condition — anyone else wants to join is welcome, good learning opportunity.

metadata.notes: Marina on PTO. Ivan participated actively — 285 words, proactively identified a risk, offered to sync with Tom. Team energy positive. Ivan showing technical leadership.
